title: Finding the mean of a dataset
problem: Find the mean of the dataset: $5, \ 8, \ 3, \ 10, \ 4$.

steps.instruction: Add all the values to find the sum.
Count the number of values in the dataset.
Divide the sum by the number of values.

title: Finding the median and mode
problem: For the dataset $7, \ 3, \ 9, \ 3, \ 5, \ 11, \ 3, \ 8$, find (a) the median and (b) the mode.

steps.instruction: Order the values from smallest to largest.
Find the median. With $8$ values (an even count), average the $4$th and $5$th values.
Find the mode by identifying the most frequently occurring value.
steps.working: $3$ appears $3$ times; all other values appear fewer times. $\text{Mode} = 3$

title: Finding the mean from a frequency table
problem:
The frequency table below shows the number of siblings reported by $20$ students.

$$\begin{array}{|c|c|}\hline \text{Number of siblings} & \text{Frequency} \\ \hline 0 & 5 \\ 1 & 8 \\ 2 & 4 \\ 3 & 3 \\ \hline \end{array}$$

Find the mean number of siblings.

steps.instruction: Multiply each value by its frequency to find the contribution of each row.
Add the products to find the total number of siblings across all students.
Add the frequencies to confirm the total number of students.
Divide the total by the total frequency to find the mean.
